heading: Authentication with AWS IAM
body:
You can authenticate applications running on AWS (Amazon Web Services) services to Atlas
clusters with AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) roles. You can set up a database user to
use an AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) role ARN for authentication and connect to your
database using [mongosh](mongodb-shell) and drivers that authenticate using your AWS (Amazon Web Services)
IAM (Identity and Access Management) role ARN. Using AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) role reduces the number of
authentication mechanisms and number of secrets to manage.

To learn more, see [AWS IAM Authentication](set-up-pwdless-auth).
